topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #4 Tier 1 (WR3, PHI) AND RSP DoT 88.8 Starter grade with Amon-Ra St. Brown comp — both primary boards converge, which my 50/25/25 weighting rewards heavily. ADP 11.18 confirms market value at 1.03; safest Tier 1 WR likely on the board after Love/Tate go 1.01-1.02.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #3 Tier 1 (WR2, NO) with ADP 12.71 — Consensus and ADP both rank him ahead of Lemon, but RSP DoT 84.5 'boom-bust volatility' is a yellow flag. At medium RSP affinity I won't fully discount Waldman's caution, so he ranks behind Lemon despite higher Consensus slot.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #21 Tier 3 (TE2, PHI) and RSP DoT 84.4 'on cusp of Starter tier' — best-available TE at 2.03 in the dead zone between Sadiq (gone top 6) and Klare (#25). My TE room is 4 deep but aging; Stowers is a long-term plug at a thin position with two boards in agreement.

topTargets.name: Chris Bell
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #19 Tier 2 (WR10, MIA), RSP DoT 83.9 with A.J. Brown high-end comp, ADP 28.24. Falls right at 2.03 with Tier 2 value still available. RSP says 'wait for second round if falls' — that's exactly the slot I'm picking.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WR14, WAS), RSP DoT 83.9 with Jayden Reed comp — RSP flags as 'potential bargain' and ADP 29.06 confirms. Perfect 3.03 lottery ticket where RSP and ADP both see late-round value.

capPosture: Comfortable at $34.5M of $45M ($10.5M space) with 6 contracts expiring — can active-roster all three rookies without forcing taxi, but will likely taxi the 3rd-rounder for the 50% discount.

wildcard.name: Skyler Bell
wildcard.reasoning: RSP DoT 85.9 Starter grade with Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp — Waldman's biggest RSP-Consensus delta (RSP #6 vs Consensus #33 FA tier). At medium affinity I won't reach in round 1, but at 3.03 he's the exact dart RSP rewards.

summary: Cowboy Up enters the draft with three picks at the 0.03 slot, $10.5M cap room, and a balanced 27-man roster — no desperate need to reach. Medium RSP affinity means I anchor on Consensus Tier 1 at 1.03 (Lemon over Tyson because RSP confirms) then hunt Tier 2/3 WR with both-board agreement at 2.03 and a small RSP-driven lottery ticket at 3.03. Goal: add 2-3 starting-caliber pass catchers to a roster with 6 contracts expiring this year.
